R Notebook

library(lme4)
library(MAST)
library(data.table)
library(R.utils)
library(doParallel)
library(stringr)

Processing data

fnm1 = "GSE120575_Sade_Feldman_melanoma_single_cells_nolabel_1.txt.gz"
fnm2 = "GSE120575_Sade_Feldman_melanoma_single_cells_2.txt.gz"

sf_tpm1 = fread(paste0("../../scRNAseq/Sade_Feldman_2018/", fnm1), 
                fill = TRUE, header = TRUE)

sf_tpm2 = fread(paste0("../../scRNAseq/Sade_Feldman_2018/", fnm2), 
                fill = TRUE, drop = 16293, col.names = colnames(sf_tpm1))

ls_tpm = list(sf_tpm1,sf_tpm2)
rm(sf_tpm1)
rm(sf_tpm2)

sf_tpm = rbindlist(ls_tpm)

rm(ls_tpm)
dim(sf_tpm) # 55737 x 16292
## [1] 55737 16292
sf_tpm[1:2,1:4]
gene.names = sf_tpm$V1
rownames(sf_tpm) = gene.names
sf_tpm$V1 = NULL

Only select classified cells

cell_type = read.table("./output/cell_type.txt", header = TRUE, 
                       sep = "\t", as.is = TRUE)
dim(cell_type)
## [1] 16291     4
cell_type[1:2,]
table(cell_type$type, useNA="ifany")
## 
##               B_cells           CD4T_memory                CD8T_B 
##                  1421                  1099                  2458 
##                CD8T_G       Dendritic_cells Monocytes_Macrophages 
##                  3033                   275                  1341 
##                   NK1                   NK2                   NK3 
##                   463                  1904                  1146 
##          Plasma_cells                 Tregs           unclustered 
##                   290                  1313                  1548
clustered_cells = cell_type$cell[cell_type$type != "unclustered"]
cluster_sf_tpm  = sf_tpm[, ..clustered_cells]
dim(cluster_sf_tpm)
## [1] 55737 14743
rownames(cluster_sf_tpm) = gene.names
cluster_sf_tpm[1:3,1:3]
rm(sf_tpm)
rm(clustered_cells)

Select ~10,000 genes expressed in at least a proportion of cells

Calculate the proportion of cells that each gene is expressed in

cells.per.gene    = rowSums(cluster_sf_tpm > 0)
cellprop.per.gene = cells.per.gene/ncol(cluster_sf_tpm)
summary(cellprop.per.gene)
##      Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. 
## 0.0000000 0.0002713 0.0018314 0.0436938 0.0233331 1.0000000
table(cellprop.per.gene > 0.02)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 41155 14582
table(cellprop.per.gene > 0.03)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 42873 12864
table(cellprop.per.gene > 0.04)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 44144 11593
table(cellprop.per.gene > 0.05)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 45096 10641
he.genes = gene.names[cellprop.per.gene > 0.05]
HEgenes_cluster_tpm = cluster_sf_tpm[cellprop.per.gene > 0.05, ]
rownames(HEgenes_cluster_tpm) = he.genes
dim(HEgenes_cluster_tpm)
## [1] 10641 14743
summary(cellprop.per.gene[cellprop.per.gene > 0.05])
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
## 0.05006 0.08757 0.14726 0.20696 0.25883 1.00000
rm(cellprop.per.gene)
rm(cells.per.gene)
rm(cluster_sf_tpm)

Average expression of each gene in each cell type

First calculate the expression of each gene across all cells

all.expression = rowSums(HEgenes_cluster_tpm)

u_CT1: average expression of each gene in cell type CT1. o_CT1: average expression of each gene in all the cells that do not belong to cell type CT1.

average_expression = data.frame(gene = he.genes)
levels(factor(cell_type$type))
##  [1] "B_cells"               "CD4T_memory"           "CD8T_B"               
##  [4] "CD8T_G"                "Dendritic_cells"       "Monocytes_Macrophages"
##  [7] "NK1"                   "NK2"                   "NK3"                  
## [10] "Plasma_cells"          "Tregs"                 "unclustered"
for (i in levels(factor(cell_type$type))[-12]) {
  # select cells in each cell cluster
  cc = cell_type$cell[cell_type$type == i] 
  # total expression of each gene across all the cells of a particular cluster
  cluster.exp = apply(HEgenes_cluster_tpm[, ..cc], 1, sum)
  # total expression of each gene across all other cells
  exclu.exp = all.expression - cluster.exp
  # divide by the number of cells in the cluster to get the average expression
  aveg.cluster.exp = cluster.exp / length(cc)
  # divide by the number of all other cells to get the average expression
  aveg.exclu.exp = exclu.exp / (ncol(HEgenes_cluster_tpm) - length(cc))
  
  fc = aveg.exclu.exp / aveg.cluster.exp
  
  ulabel = paste0("u_",i)
  average_expression[[ulabel]] = aveg.cluster.exp
  
  olabel = paste0("o_",i)
  average_expression[[olabel]] = aveg.exclu.exp
  
  fclabel = paste0("fc_",i)
  average_expression[[fclabel]] = fc
}

rm(cc)
rm(cluster.exp)
rm(exclu.exp)
rm(aveg.cluster.exp)
rm(aveg.exclu.exp)
rm(fc)
for(i in 1:5){ gc() }
gc()
##             used   (Mb) gc trigger   (Mb) limit (Mb)   max used    (Mb)
## Ncells   5811484  310.4    8942014  477.6         NA    8133595   434.4
## Vcells 258069625 1969.0  714846216 5453.9      32768 2726921859 20804.8
write.table(average_expression, quote = FALSE, 
            file = "output/Average_GeneExpression_by_CellType.txt", 
            sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, col.names = TRUE)

Testing differential gene expression

Loading sample/patient information

dir = "../../scRNAseq/Sade_Feldman_2018/"
fnm = paste0(dir, "GSE120575_patient_ID_single_cells_rm_headers.txt")

info.SF = fread(fnm)
dim(info.SF)
## [1] 16291    35
info.SF[1:2,]
names(info.SF) = gsub("characteristics: ", "", names(info.SF))
names(info.SF)[5] = "sampleID"

all(is.na(c(data.matrix(info.SF[,8:35]))))
## [1] TRUE
info.SF = info.SF[,1:7]

pIDs = str_split_fixed(info.SF$sampleID, "_", 2)
dim(pIDs)
## [1] 16291     2
pIDs[1:2,]
##      [,1]  [,2]
## [1,] "Pre" "P1"
## [2,] "Pre" "P1"
info.SF$patientID = pIDs[,2]
info.SF$type = pIDs[,1]

dim(info.SF)
## [1] 16291     9
info.SF[1:2,]
length(unique(info.SF$title))
## [1] 16291
length(unique(info.SF$patientID))
## [1] 37
info.SF.ind = unique(info.SF[,.(patientID, sampleID, type, response, therapy)])
dim(info.SF.ind)
## [1] 48  5
info.SF.ind[1:2,]
table(info.SF.ind$response, info.SF.ind$therapy)
##                
##                 anti-CTLA4 anti-CTLA4+PD1 anti-PD1
##   Non-responder          1              4       26
##   Responder              1              7        9
table(info.SF.ind$response, info.SF.ind$type)
##                
##                 Post Pre
##   Non-responder   21  10
##   Responder        8   9
sort(table(info.SF$sampleID))
## 
##    Pre_P29   Post_P21     Pre_P1    Post_P7    Pre_P35     Pre_P3   Post_P22 
##        163        213        229        231        238        245        260 
##     Pre_P8   Post_P17     Pre_P6    Post_P1    Post_P5   Post_P15    Pre_P15 
##        267        281        288        291        292        304        304 
##     Pre_P4   Post_P11   Post_P12    Pre_P20   Post_P14    Pre_P12    Pre_P26 
##        311        313        323        323        329        330        333 
##  Post_P5_2     Pre_P2    Pre_P28    Pre_P24    Pre_P27    Pre_P31  Post_P1_2 
##        336        337        337        338        343        351        353 
##   Post_P18   Post_P20   Post_P23  Post_P3_2    Post_P3 Post_P23_2     Pre_P7 
##        357        358        358        358        359        362        368 
##   Post_P28   Post_P30    Pre_P25 Post_P28_2    Post_P2   Post_P10    Post_P6 
##        369        369        371        372        380        396        400 
##   Post_P19    Post_P8   Post_P13    Pre_P33   Post_P16    Post_P4 
##        403        409        449        452        463        675
table(tapply(info.SF.ind$type, info.SF.ind$patientID, paste, collapse=":"))
## 
##     Post      Pre Pre:Post 
##       18        8       11

Prepare gene expression data

The tutorial for MAST mentioned that the data should be a matrix of log2 + 1 transcripts per million (TPM). The data from Sade-Feldman et al. (2018) are in TPM format, so I transfromed it with log2(x+1):

table(names(HEgenes_cluster_tpm) %in% info.SF$title)
## 
##  TRUE 
## 14743
info.SF_HEgenes = info.SF[match(names(HEgenes_cluster_tpm), info.SF$title),]
dim(info.SF_HEgenes)
## [1] 14743     9
info.SF_HEgenes[1:2,]
sort(table(info.SF_HEgenes$patientID))
## 
##   P29   P21   P35   P22   P11    P5   P17   P14  P5_2   P31   P27   P24 P23_2 
##   156   203   214   222   235   259   273   286   309   310   312   316   317 
##   P26   P18  P1_2   P30   P10   P23   P25 P28_2  P3_2   P19   P33   P13   P16 
##   320   326   329   330   332   337   340   344   351   390   399   408   429 
##    P1    P7   P15   P12    P3    P8   P20    P2   P28    P6    P4 
##   452   525   543   567   569   630   635   644   657   667   807
HEgenes_cluster_tpm_log = log2(1 + as.matrix(HEgenes_cluster_tpm))

row.names(HEgenes_cluster_tpm_log) = he.genes
cell.id = colnames(HEgenes_cluster_tpm_log)

dim(HEgenes_cluster_tpm_log)
## [1] 10641 14743
HEgenes_cluster_tpm_log[1:2,1:5]
##       A10_P3_M11 A11_P1_M11 A11_P3_M11 A11_P4_M11 A12_P3_M11
## DPM1           0   3.356144          0   3.168321          0
## SCYL3          0   0.000000          0   0.000000          0

Calculating the CDR rate: proportion of genes detected for each cell

cdr = colMeans(HEgenes_cluster_tpm > 0)
summary(cdr)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
## 0.08448 0.15356 0.18523 0.20696 0.23447 0.67569

Preparing a SingleCellAssay object for MAST

fData = data.frame(primerid = he.genes)
cData = data.frame(wellKey = cell.id)

sca = FromMatrix(HEgenes_cluster_tpm_log, cData, fData) 
## Assuming data assay in position 1, with name et is log-transformed.
colData(sca)$cngeneson = scale(cdr)
colData(sca)$patientID = info.SF_HEgenes$patientID

uniq_cell_types = sort(unique(cell_type$type))

for(i in 1:11){
  label = uniq_cell_types[i]
  colData(sca)[[label]] = "control"
  caseid = match(cell_type$cell[cell_type$type == label],colData(sca)$wellKey)
  colData(sca)[[label]][caseid] = "case"
  colData(sca)[[label]] = as.factor(colData(sca)[[label]])
}

colData(sca)
## DataFrame with 14743 rows and 14 columns
##                                             wellKey          cngeneson
##                                         <character>           <matrix>
## A10_P3_M11                               A10_P3_M11 -0.425092173776452
## A11_P1_M11                               A11_P1_M11 -0.839262909803626
## A11_P3_M11                               A11_P3_M11  -0.83690966698529
## A11_P4_M11                               A11_P4_M11  0.587978859517289
## A12_P3_M11                               A12_P3_M11  -1.27343620978666
## ...                                             ...                ...
## H5_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched H5_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched   2.04345954265824
## H6_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched H6_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched   2.62941700442396

## H8_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched H8_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched  -1.13106501927732
## H9_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched H9_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched -0.813377238801928
##                             patientID  B_cells CD4T_memory   CD8T_B   CD8T_G
##                           <character> <factor>    <factor> <factor> <factor>
## A10_P3_M11                         P1  control     control  control  control
## A11_P1_M11                         P1  control     control  control  control
## A11_P3_M11                         P1  control     control  control  control
## A11_P4_M11                         P1  control     control  control  control
## A12_P3_M11                         P1  control     control  control  control
## ...                               ...      ...         ...      ...      ...
## H5_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched          P6  control     control  control  control
## H6_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched          P6  control     control  control  control

## H8_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched          P6  control     control  control  control
## H9_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched          P6  control     control  control  control
##                           Dendritic_cells Monocytes_Macrophages      NK1
##                                  <factor>              <factor> <factor>
## A10_P3_M11                        control               control  control
## A11_P1_M11                        control               control  control
## A11_P3_M11                        control               control  control
## A11_P4_M11                        control               control  control
## A12_P3_M11                        control               control  control
## ...                                   ...                   ...      ...
## H5_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched         control               control     case
## H6_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched         control               control     case
## H7_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched         control               control  control
## H8_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched         control               control  control
## H9_P5_M67_L001_T_enriched         control               control  control
##                                NK2      NK3 Plasma_cells    Tregs
##                           <factor> <factor>     <factor> <factor>
## A10_P3_M11                    case  control      control  control
## A11_P1_M11                    case  control      control  control
## A11_P3_M11                    case  control      control  control
## A11_P4_M11                 control  control      control     case
## A12_P3_M11                    case  control      control  control
## ...                            ...      ...          ...      ...

rm(HEgenes_cluster_tpm)
rm(HEgenes_cluster_tpm_log)
for(i in 1:5){ gc() }
gc()
##             used   (Mb) gc trigger   (Mb) limit (Mb)   max used    (Mb)
## Ncells   5845735  312.2    8942014  477.6         NA    8263390   441.4
## Vcells 258768629 1974.3  742180547 5662.4      32768 2726921859 20804.8

Fitting model, for one cell type to compare standard model vs. mixed effect model

First try to fit two models, one is the standard MAST model and the other one is a mixed effect model that account for the dependency of cells that are from the same patient. For this dataset with around 10,000 genes and 14,743 cells, MAST will could take around 8G memory per thread. With 5 threads, zlm without mixed effect model takes about 7 minutes, and subsequent lrt takes about 6 minutes. zlm with mixed effect takes about 1 hour, and subsequent lrt takes about 37 minutes.

# number of cores for multi-core computation
nCore = 5
registerDoParallel(cores=nCore)
options(mc.cores=nCore)
getOption("mc.cores")
## [1] 5
i = 1
cti = uniq_cell_types[i]
f0  = as.formula(sprintf("~ %s + cngeneson", cti))
f1  = as.formula(sprintf("~ %s + cngeneson + (1|patientID)", cti))

date()
## [1] "Thu Nov 26 14:49:51 2020"
b0  = zlm(formula = f0, sca = sca, parallel = TRUE) 
## 
## Done!
date()
## [1] "Thu Nov 26 14:53:13 2020"
b1 = zlm(formula = f1, sca=sca, method = "glmer", ebayes=F, parallel = T)
## 
## Done!
## Warning in rbind(...): number of columns of result is not a multiple of vector
## length (arg 4719)
date()
## [1] "Thu Nov 26 15:44:42 2020"
lrt0 = lrTest(b0, cti)
## Refitting on reduced model...
## 
## Done!
date()
## [1] "Thu Nov 26 15:50:27 2020"
lrt1 = lrTest(b1, cti)
## Refitting on reduced model...
## 
## Done!
date()
## [1] "Thu Nov 26 16:25:57 2020"
mast_pval_zlm  = apply(lrt0, 1, function(x){x[3,3]})
mas_pval_zlmer = apply(lrt1, 1, function(x){x[3,3]})

summary(mast_pval_zlm)
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median     Mean  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## 0.000000 0.000000 0.001818 0.135111 0.149785 0.999647
summary(mas_pval_zlmer)
##      Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max.      NA's 
## 0.0000000 0.0000001 0.0090784 0.1598833 0.2222523 1.0000000         1
min(mast_pval_zlm)
## [1] 0
min(mast_pval_zlm[mast_pval_zlm > 0])
## [1] 1.770237e-320
table(mast_pval_zlm < 1e-100)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 10229   412
table(mas_pval_zlmer < 1e-100)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 10341   299
mast_pval_zlm[which(mast_pval_zlm < 1e-100)] = 1e-100
mas_pval_zlmer[which(mas_pval_zlmer < 1e-100)] = 1e-100

cor(-log10(mast_pval_zlm), -log10(mas_pval_zlmer), use="pairwise")
## [1] 0.9710617
plot(-log10(mast_pval_zlm), -log10(mas_pval_zlmer), pch=20, cex=0.5)
abline(0, 1, col="orange", lwd=2)

plot(-log10(mast_pval_zlm), -log10(mas_pval_zlmer), xlim=c(0,10), ylim=c(0,10), pch=20, cex=0.5)
abline(0, 1, col="orange", lwd=2)

Fitting model, for all other cell types

# number of cores for multi-core computation
nCore = 5
registerDoParallel(cores=nCore)
options(mc.cores=nCore)
getOption("mc.cores")
## [1] 5
mast_pval_zlm_df = fData
names(mast_pval_zlm_df)[1] = "gene"

for(i in 1:11){
  cti = uniq_cell_types[i]

  cat(i, cti, date(), "\n")
  f0  = as.formula(sprintf("~ %s + cngeneson", cti))
  b0  = zlm(formula = f0, sca = sca, parallel = TRUE) 
  cat("start lrtest", date(), "\n")

  lrt0 = lrTest(b0, cti)

  mast_pval_zlm_df[[cti]]  = apply(lrt0, 1, function(x){x[3,3]})
  gc()
}
## 1 B_cells Thu Nov 26 16:25:59 2020
## 
## Done!

## Refitting on reduced model...
## 
## Done!
## 10 Plasma_cells Thu Nov 26 17:31:32 2020
## 
## Done!
## start lrtest Thu Nov 26 17:35:27 2020
## Refitting on reduced model...
## 
## Done!
## 11 Tregs Thu Nov 26 17:38:57 2020
## 
## Done!
## start lrtest Thu Nov 26 17:45:18 2020
## Refitting on reduced model...
## 
## Done!
dim(mast_pval_zlm_df)
## [1] 10641    12
mast_pval_zlm_df[1:2,]
write.csv(mast_pval_zlm_df, file = "output/MastGenePvals_by_CellType.csv", 
          quote = FALSE)
gc()
##             used   (Mb) gc trigger   (Mb) limit (Mb)   max used    (Mb)
## Ncells   5999604  320.5   11630125  621.2         NA   11630125   621.2
## Vcells 262142070 2000.0  872381049 6655.8      32768 2726921859 20804.8
sessionInfo()

##  [1] Rcpp_1.0.3             lattice_0.20-38        prettyunits_1.1.1     
##  [4] assertthat_0.2.1       digest_0.6.23          R6_2.4.1
